perturbation.original_text: 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee, and Licensee hereby accepts from Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark solely and exclusively as a component of Licensee's own corporate name and in connection with marketing the investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services that Investment Advisor may provide to Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this License, and except as provided above, neither Licensee nor any affiliate, owner, director, officer, employee or agent thereof shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivative thereof in the Territory without the prior express written consent of Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion and shall not use the Licensed Mark for any purpose outside the Territory. All rights not expressly granted to Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee, and Licensee hereby accepts from Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark solely as a component of Licensee's own corporate name. During the term of this Agreement, Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this License, and except as provided above, neither Licensee nor any affiliate, owner, director, officer, employee or agent thereof shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivative thereof in the Territory without the prior express written consent of Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion and shall not use the Licensed Mark for any purpose outside the Territory. All rights not expressly granted to Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and in connection with marketing the investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services that Investment Advisor may provide to Licensee' from section 1.1, it introduces an ambiguity. This is because in other parts of the agreement, such as the Recitals section, the purpose of the license is described to include marketing services, contradicting the limited scope defined in the modified section 1.1. This creates a contradiction in the intended scope of the license grant within different sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECITALS WHEREAS, Licensee is a newly organized, externally managed, closed-end, non-diversified management investment company that intends to elect to be regulated as a business development company under the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended (the "1940 Act"); WHEREAS, Licensor, together with its affiliates, provides investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services; WHEREAS, Licensor and its affiliates, including Palmer Square BDC Advisor LLC, a Delaware limited liability company ("Investment Advisor"), have used the marks "Palmer Square", "Palmer Square Capital" and derivations thereof (the "Licensed Mark") in the United States of America and certain other jurisdictions (collectively, the "Territory") in connection with the investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services they provide; WHEREAS, the Licensee is entering into an investment advisory agreement with Investment Advisor (the "Advisory Agreement"), wherein Licensee shall engage Investment Advisor to act as the investment adviser to the Licensee; WHEREAS, it is intended that Investment Advisor be a third party beneficiary of this Agreement; and WHEREAS, the Licensee desires to use the Licensed Mark as part of its corporate name and in connection with the operation of its business, and Licensor is willing to grant Licensee a license to use the Licensed Mark, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 1.1 explicitly grants the Licensee the right to use the Licensed Mark "in connection with marketing the investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services." The changed text removes this explicit grant. This directly contradicts the Recitals section, which states, "WHEREAS, the Licensee desires to use the Licensed Mark as part of its corporate name and in connection with the operation of its business." Since 'operation of its business' as described in the recitals, explicitly includes investment related activities, the removal of the investment related activity right creates a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1. Quality Control. In order to preserve the inherent value of the Licensed Mark, Licensee agrees to use reasonable efforts to ensure that it maintains the quality of the Licensee's business and the operation thereof equal to the standards prevailing in the operation of Licensee's business as of the Effective Date. The Licensee further agrees to use the Licensed Mark in accordance with such quality standards as may be reasonably established by Licensor and communicated to the Licensee from time to time in writing, or as may be agreed to by Licensor and the Licensee from time to time in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1. Quality Control. Licensee agrees to use its best efforts to promote the Licensed Mark in a positive manner. The Licensee further agrees to use the Licensed Mark in accordance with such quality standards as may be reasonably established by Licensor and communicated to the Licensee from time to time in writing, or as may be agreed to by Licensor and the Licensee from time to time in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Licensee agrees to use reasonable efforts to ensure that it maintains the quality of the Licensee's business and the operation thereof equal to the standards prevailing in the operation of Licensee's business as of the Effective Date' to 'Licensee agrees to use its best efforts to promote the Licensed Mark in a positive manner' introduces ambiguity. The original text implied a maintenance of existing quality standards, while the new text suggests proactive promotion. This creates an internal contradiction with the section where Licensee accepts the license on an "as is" basis, thus this section is expecting the license on how it already it is. This creates contradiction and uncertainty in the quality control expectations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1. Licensee accepts this license on an "as is" basis. Licensee acknowledges that Licensor makes no explicit or implicit representation or warranty as to the registrability, validity, enforceability, ownership of the Licensed Mark, or as to Licensee's ability to use the Licensed Mark without infringing or otherwise violating the rights of others, and Licensor has no obligation to indemnify Licensee with respect to any claims arising from Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark, including without limitation any Third Party Claim.
perturbation.justification: The original text emphasizes maintaining existing quality standards tied to the business's performance as of the effective date. The changed text shifts the focus to 'best efforts to promote the Licensed Mark in a positive manner,' which introduces a proactive obligation. This contrasts directly with section 3.1, where the Licensee accepts the license on an 'as is' basis, meaning without any assurance of quality or value. The requirement to actively promote is a separate obligation from the license, which creates an in-text contradiction between proactive improvements and as-is acceptance, fulfilling the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1. Term. This Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement shall be terminable by Licensor at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that Licensor or Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark; by Licensor or Licensee upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the other party; or by Licensee at any time in the event Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of Licensor.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1. Term. This Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement shall be terminable by Licensor or Licensee upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the other party; or by Licensee at any time in the event Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or one of its affiliates' and 'at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that Licensor or Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark', we introduce an ambiguity. The contract now lacks clarity on whether termination is triggered if any affiliate of the Investment Advisor ceases to serve as the investment advisor and in what instances can be terminated by Licensor. This creates an internal contradiction with the term stating that Licensee accepts this license on an "as is" basis.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1. Licensee accepts this license on an "as is" basis. Licensee acknowledges that Licensor makes no explicit or implicit representation or warranty as to the registrability, validity, enforceability, ownership of the Licensed Mark, or as to Licensee's ability to use the Licensed Mark without infringing or otherwise violating the rights of others, and Licensor has no obligation to indemnify Licensee with respect to any claims arising from Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark, including without limitation any Third Party Claim.
perturbation.justification: The removal of specific termination triggers, namely 'or one of its affiliates' and the clause regarding Third Party Claims, introduces ambiguity and limitations on the Licensor's right to terminate. This directly contradicts the clause where the Licensee accepts the license on an 'as is' basis. The 'as is' clause suggests acceptance of all risks associated with the license, but the modified termination clause limits the Licensor's ability to mitigate those risks, creating a direct conflict in the understanding and enforcement of responsibilities. The change made follows the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction, specifically, 'Ambiguities occur when key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently in different parts of a contract, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear.'
